name: Debbi's German Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 sticks unsalted butter (12 tablespoons), room temperature
recipeInstructions:
Cake Directions:
Butter and flour 3 nine-inch cake pans. Preheat oven to 350°F.
Grate 4 ounces of the German's chocolate and set aside. Blend the remaining 4 ounces of German's Chocolate with the boiling water in a small bowl or measuring cup; set aside to cool slightly.
In a large bowl, whisk together and blend well the flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
With an electric mixer, blend 1 1/2 sticks butter, sugar; add egg yolks one at a time, blending well after each addition. Add vanilla and continue to blend. Add mashed potatoes, beating in. Pour in chocolate-water mixture and blend well. Blend slowly at first, then speed up as additions are made, alternating between adding dry ingredients and buttermilk about one-third of the way. End with dry ingredients.
In a glass, copper, or stainless steel bowl, beat three egg whites to form stiff peaks. Gently fold egg whites and grated chocolate into batter to blend completely. Distribute batter in three prepared pans as evenly as possible. Bake 20 to 25 minutes at 350°F. Remove when done. Cool on a wire rack.
Frosting Directions:
Remove 1/2 cup of toasted pecans and 2 tablespoons of toasted coconut, set aside for garnish.
In a saucepan over medium heat, mix evaporated milk and 2 1/4 sticks butter (18 tablespoons). Whisk and melt until thoroughly blended. Add sugar and whisk to blend and cook down the sugar. Whisk briskly so as not to cook the eggs, add egg yolks one at a time. Add vanilla and mix well. Cook over low-medium heat for about 12 minutes until it has a rich caramel color and slightly cools. Stir in coconut and toasted pecans; blend well.
Finishing cake:
Stack layers, using a thin layer of frosting as filling. Frost entire cake. Use remaining pecans to evenly press into the sides of the cake all around. Place pecan halves around the edge of the top. Place coconut in the center of the top.
